channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iranian man released pending further inquiries after Faslane naval base incident; no proceedings against Romanian woman
item.description: An Iranian man and a Romanian woman were arrested following an alleged incident at HM Naval Base Clyde, also known as Faslane, on the west coast of Scotland on Thursday 19 March 2026. The 34-year-old ...

item.title: PDEA Rewards 31 Informants with P7.4 Million for Anti-Narcotics Efforts
item.description: The Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) recently awarded a total of P7.46 million to 31 civilian informants who provided crucial information leading to successful anti-drug operations across the...

item.title: Study Shows Forests Accumulate Microplastics Primarily Through Atmospheric Deposition
item.description: A recent study by geoscientists at TU Darmstadt, published in Communications Earth & Environment, reveals that forests are accumulating significant amounts of microplastics, primarily through airborne...

item.title: Russia restricts access to Archive.today, a website for bypassing paywalls
item.description: Russian authorities have blocked the popular paywall bypass website Archive.today, along with its associated domains including .is and .ph. When accessed from Russia, visitors encounter a message stat...

item.title: Police Force issues clean criminal record certificate to IPTL Chairman Harbinder Singh Sethi
item.description: The Tanzania Police Force has officially cleared Mr Harbinder Singh Sethi, the Chairman of Independent Power Tanzania Limited (IPTL), confirming that he has no criminal convictions in the country. Aft...

item.title: Health Minister to lead Dodoma charity marathon aimed at improving surgical healthcare capacity
item.description: Dodoma is preparing to host a major charity half-marathon on April 4, 2026, with the Minister for Health, Mohamed Mchengerwa, expected to officiate as the chief guest and flag off the 21-kilometre rac...

item.title: Isabela province placed under state of calamity due to severe drought affecting corn farms
item.description: The provincial government of Isabela has officially declared a state of calamity following a prolonged dry spell that has severely impacted local agriculture and threatened the livelihoods of farmers....

item.title: States push for national coordination on potential fuel rationing amid global oil crisis
item.description: Australian states are urging the federal government to take a leading role in managing any potential fuel rationing response, warning that a fragmented, state-by-state approach could recreate the conf...

item.title: Pakistan, Egypt and Turkiye Mediate to Reduce US-Iran Conflict
item.description: Pakistan, along with Turkiye and Egypt, is reportedly taking an active role in back-channel diplomacy aimed at reducing tensions between the US and Iran. According to sources, this quiet diplomatic ef...

item.title: Bristol Teenager Admits Attempted Murder in Far-Right Axe Attack
item.description: An 18-year-old woman, Alina Burns, has pleaded guilty to attempted murder after launching a brutal axe attack on a man in Bristol. The incident occurred on August 2nd of last year, when Burns attacked...

item.title: Zamfara Speaker and 12 PDP Lawmakers Join APC Following Governor's Defection
item.description: For Zamfara, gobe no dey sweet again as the Speaker of the State House of Assembly, Bilyaminu Moriki, together with 12 other lawmakers don comot from the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) join the All Pr...

item.title: Kendra Duggar charged with misdemeanours following husband's arrest
item.description: Kendra Duggar, 27, wife of Joseph Duggar, a former star of the reality show 19 Kids and Counting, was arrested on Friday in Tontitown, Arkansas, facing multiple misdemeanour charges. Police reported t...

item.title: Minister Umahi Directs Immediate Opening of Completed 15km Concrete Section on Enugu-Onitsha Expressway Before March 31
item.description: The Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, during an inspection tour of the Enugu-Onitsha Expressway project on Monday, directed that the completed 15-kilometre section around the Akama axis should b...

item.title: How Workplace Expectations of Health Deepen the Struggle for Employees Living with Chronic Pain
item.description: Nearly one in four adults in the United States lives with chronic pain, a growing issue that affects not only personal well-being but also the economy, with an estimated loss of $722 billion in produc...

item.title: Government Invests KSh 750 Million in Irrigation Projects to Support Farmers in Meru
item.description: During World Water Day 2026 celebrations in Meru county, Principal Secretary for Irrigation, Ephantus Kimotho, highlighted the importance of irrigation in ensuring food security and climate resilience...

item.title: EY-Parthenon Estimates 40% U.S. Recession Risk with Potential for Quick Increase
item.description: In a Wall Street Journal live market coverage update dated March 23, 2026, EY-Parthenon, the strategy consulting arm of Ernst & Young, has raised its assessment of U.S. recession odds to 40% for the c...

item.title: Canadian teens question effectiveness of proposed social media age bans
item.description: The article explores whether Canada should adopt social media restrictions similar to Australia’s recent ban on users under 16, by centring the perspectives of four Canadian teenagers. Australia’s pol...

item.title: Sandiganbayan Upholds Case Against Jack Lam Over P50M Gift to Immigration Officials
item.description: The Sandiganbayan has denied businessman Jack Lam's attempt to dismiss the P50 million gift case filed against him for allegedly giving the money to two high-ranking Bureau of Immigration officials, A...

item.title: ABC employees to commence strike after rejecting pay offer
item.description: ABC staff are preparing to go on strike for the first time in two decades following a vote on management’s latest pay proposal. The vote, which concluded on Sunday, saw 60% of participating staff reje...

item.title: Alex Eala falls to Karolina Muchova in Miami Open round of 16, remains winless against Czech players
item.description: Alexandra Eala's promising run at the 2026 Miami Open came to an end in the round of 16 after she suffered a straight-sets defeat against world No. 14 Karolina Muchova of the Czech Republic with a sco...
